core_mechanism: a phase transition in the eigenvalue (spectral) density of a large-dimension operator/ensemble as its size grows, captured by a single universal scaling function obtained in a double-scaling limit (tuning size and a control parameter together), with the same scaling function defining a universality class shared across otherwise distinct systems
brief_reason: The abstract derives a large N scaling function in a double scaling limit for the transition in a Wilson loop operator's eigenvalue distribution and hypothesizes that other cases fall in the same universality class, i.e. a size-driven spectral transition governed by a universal double-scaling crossover function.
